# nuthatch CLI reference

<!-- GENERATED by `nuthatch skill-refs` from clap metadata ‚Äî do not edit by hand. -->

Every subcommand and flag the `nuthatch` binary exposes. Regenerate with `nuthatch skill-refs`.

## `nuthatch add`

Add another contract to an existing nest ‚Äî resolve its ABI and grow the config, no re-init

- `<ADDRESSES>` ‚Äî One or more contract addresses to add to the nest, e.g. 0xC02a‚Ä¶6Cc2 (WETH)
- `--alias <ALIAS>` ‚Äî Optional aliases, one per address in order (comma-separated). Defaults to the next free c<N> slots after the nest's existing contracts
- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî The nest directory to grow (must contain a nuthatch.toml). Defaults to the current directory
- `--rpc <RPC>` ‚Äî Prefer these RPC URL(s) over the nest's configured endpoints for ABI/deploy-block resolution (repeatable). Point at your own node to dodge public-RPC limits

## `nuthatch audit`

Audit the compliance annotations: `replay` re-proves them, `report` summarises them (C6)


## `nuthatch audit replay`

Re-run screening over the sealed segments and confirm the stored hits reproduce exactly

- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Nest directory
- `--from <FROM>` ‚Äî First block of the range (inclusive)
- `--to <TO>` ‚Äî Last block of the range (inclusive)

## `nuthatch audit report`

Summarise the hits and flags in a block range (markdown or `--json`)

- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Nest directory
- `--from <FROM>` ‚Äî First block of the range (inclusive)
- `--to <TO>` ‚Äî Last block of the range (inclusive)
- `--json` ‚Äî Emit JSON instead of markdown

## `nuthatch bench`

Benchmark the indexing pipeline (measure first, optimise second ‚Äî RFC-0004)


## `nuthatch bench backfill`

Measure backfill throughput (events/sec, wall-clock, peak RSS) over a pinned block range

- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Nest directory (must contain a `nuthatch.toml`)
- `--from <FROM>` ‚Äî First block of the pinned range (inclusive)
- `--to <TO>` ‚Äî Last block of the pinned range (inclusive)
- `--runs <RUNS>` ‚Äî How many runs to take (the report is the median). Public RPC is noisy ‚Äî 3 is sensible
- `--rpc <RPC>` ‚Äî Override the nest's `rpc_urls` (e.g. point at your own archive node for a T2 run)
- `--out <OUT>` ‚Äî Write the bench-report JSON here (e.g. `docs/bench/w1.json`). Prints to stdout regardless
- `--label <LABEL>` ‚Äî A label for the workload in the report (e.g. "W1: USDC 100k dense")
- `--seal-direct` ‚Äî Measure the seal-direct path (decode ‚Üí Parquet, bypassing the hot store) instead of the default decode ‚Üí redb hot-store path. Use to compare the two backfill storage paths
- `--concurrency <CONCURRENCY>` ‚Äî Concurrent window fetches (seal-direct only). >1 overlaps RPC round-trip latency; results are still consumed in block order so segments are identical. Try 8‚Äì16 against your own node

## `nuthatch bench query`

Measure the read path: entity point-read latency (p50/p99) and the `/sql` hot‚à™cold scan cost (query latency + peak RSS). The regression guard for the perf refactors ‚Äî run offline against an already-indexed nest

- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Nest directory (must contain an indexed `nuthatch.redb`). Stop `dev` first ‚Äî the bench opens the store directly
- `--sql <SQL>` ‚Äî The `/sql` query to time (over hot‚à™cold). Defaults to `SELECT count(*)` on the largest hot table ‚Äî the full-tip-materialising scan whose cost is the #1 RAM risk on deep-finality L2s
- `--reads <READS>` ‚Äî Entity point-reads to time (keys sampled evenly across the hot store)
- `--iters <ITERS>` ‚Äî `/sql` query repetitions to time (the report is the p50/p99 across them)
- `--out <OUT>` ‚Äî Write the bench-report JSON here. Prints to stdout regardless
- `--label <LABEL>` ‚Äî A label for the report (e.g. "R1: horizon tip 12k rows")

## `nuthatch check`

Run a nest's invariant/parity checks (`checks/*.sql`) against recorded expected results

- `<NAME>` ‚Äî Optional check-name filter (substring). Omit to run every `checks/*.sql`. E.g. `parity`
- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Nest directory (must contain a `checks/` folder)
- `--update` ‚Äî Record current query results as the expected fixtures (`checks/expected/*.json`) instead of comparing ‚Äî the authoring mode, run once against known-good sealed data

## `nuthatch dev`

Run the indexer: poll logs, store entities, and serve the API

- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Project directory (must contain a nuthatch.toml)
- `--listen <LISTEN>` ‚Äî Address to bind the HTTP API to
- `--rpc <RPC>` ‚Äî Override the nest's `rpc_urls` at runtime without editing the config (repeatable). These are tried first; the nest's configured endpoints remain as fallback. Point at your own node
- `--backfill <BACKFILL>` ‚Äî Index only this many blocks back from the tip (recent-history mode). Explicitly overrides a nest's vendored `start_block`s. Omit to backfill from deployment when the nest declares start blocks, else from a default recent window
- `--seal-direct` ‚Äî Backfill finalized history straight to Parquet (skip the hot store) before tip-following ‚Äî much faster for a from-deployment backfill (RFC-0004). The near-tip window still uses the hot path; the IVM view is rebuilt from the sealed segments
- `--concurrency <CONCURRENCY>` ‚Äî Concurrent window fetches during the seal-direct history backfill (overlaps RPC latency). Try 8‚Äì16 against your own node; keep low on rate-limited public RPC
- `--window <WINDOW>` ‚Äî Override the `eth_getLogs` block-window (the chain default otherwise). For a *sparse* contract over a long backfill ‚Äî few events across many blocks ‚Äî a large window (e.g. 50000) turns tens of thousands of near-empty requests into a few, so a from-history backfill finishes in minutes. Keep it under your provider's max block-range for `getLogs` (many allow 100k+ when the result set is small); the concurrent backfill fails the range rather than auto-shrinking it
- `--no-admin` ‚Äî Disable the built-in admin UI (`/_admin/`) entirely ‚Äî no routes, for hosted deployments that front their own dashboard (RFC-0010 Part A). Off-localhost the UI requires `NUTHATCH_ADMIN_TOKEN` to be set AND each request to present it as `?token=‚Ä¶` (or it self-disables with a log line)

## `nuthatch init`

Scaffold an indexer for a contract: resolve its ABI and write a project here

- `<ADDRESSES>` ‚Äî One or more contract addresses to index, e.g. 0xA0b8‚Ä¶eB48 (USDC). Omit when using `--from`
- `--from <FROM>` ‚Äî Initialise from a published nest instead of addresses: a git URL or a local directory. The nest is self-contained (ABIs vendored), so nothing is resolved ‚Äî just cloned/copied + validated
- `--alias <ALIAS>` ‚Äî Optional aliases, one per address in order (comma-separated). Defaults to c0, c1, ‚Ä¶
- `--chain <CHAIN>` ‚Äî Chain to index, e.g. mainnet, arbitrum-one, base. Omit it and nuthatch probes each known chain for the contract's bytecode and picks the one it lives on ‚Äî you rarely need to say
- `--rpc <RPC>` ‚Äî Prefer these RPC URL(s) over the chain defaults (repeatable). They're written first in the nest's `rpc_urls` and also used for ABI/deploy-block resolution during init, with the built-in chain endpoints kept as fallback. Point at your own node to dodge public-RPC limits
- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Directory to scaffold into (defaults to the current directory; for `--from`, defaults to the nest's own name)

## `nuthatch labels`

Manage labeled address sets ‚Äî the compliance annotation substrate (RFC-0008 C1)


## `nuthatch labels import`

Import a labeled address set (CSV `address,label` or JSON) as a content-addressed snapshot

- `<FILE>` ‚Äî Path to the label file: CSV (`address,label` per line, optional header) or JSON (an array of `{address,label}` objects, or an `{address: label}` map)
- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Nest directory to import into (writes `labels/<hash>.json`)

## `nuthatch labels list`

List the imported label snapshots and how many addresses each carries

- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Nest directory to read labels from

## `nuthatch lists`

Manage sanctions/watch lists as content-addressed snapshots (RFC-0008 C2)


## `nuthatch lists fetch`

Fetch a sanctions/watch list (host-side, out-of-band) into a content-addressed snapshot

- `<LIST>` ‚Äî List name. Known: `ofac-sdn`, `eu-consolidated` (have default URLs). Any other name needs `--url` or `--file`
- `--file <FILE>` ‚Äî Read the list from a local file instead of downloading (any text: XML/CSV/JSON/plain ‚Äî the fetcher extracts every `0x‚Ä¶40hex` address)
- `--url <URL>` ‚Äî Override the download URL for this list
- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Nest directory to fetch into (writes `lists/<hash>.json`)

## `nuthatch lists list`

List the fetched list snapshots and how many addresses each carries

- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Nest directory to read list snapshots from

## `nuthatch mcp`

Serve the Model Context Protocol over stdio (bridges to a running `nuthatch dev`)

- `--url <URL>` ‚Äî Base URL of the running `nuthatch dev` HTTP API to bridge to
- `--print-config` ‚Äî Print a copy-paste MCP client config (Claude Code `.mcp.json` + the `claude mcp add` one-liner) and exit, instead of running the stdio server. This is the "wire it up in one step" helper

## `nuthatch nest`

Package a nest as a content-addressed blob ‚Äî the deploy unit (RFC-0012)


## `nuthatch nest mount`

Verify a nest blob and install it as a runnable nest directory. Checks the manifest format, each file's hash, and that the decode registry regenerated from the inputs matches the manifest

- `<BLOB>` ‚Äî Blob directory to mount (a `nest pack` output)
- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Target directory to install the nest into (default: the nest's name)
- `--expect <EXPECT>` ‚Äî Assert the blob's content-address hash equals this value before installing

## `nuthatch nest pack`

Pack a nest directory into a content-addressed blob: its authored inputs (config, ABIs, views, labels, skills) plus a `manifest.json` pinning the expected decode-registry hash. Prints the blob hash ‚Äî the nest's content address

- `<DIR>` ‚Äî Nest directory to pack
- `--out <OUT>` ‚Äî Output blob directory (default: `<nest-name>-<hash>.nest` beside the nest)

## `nuthatch pack`

Build, sign, and verify the signed compliance-pack manifest (RFC-0008 C6)


## `nuthatch pack build`

Assemble `compliance-pack.toml` from the nest's config + artifact hashes (optionally signed)

- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Nest directory
- `--key <KEY>` ‚Äî Sign the manifest with this keypair file (from `pack keygen`). Omit to write it unsigned

## `nuthatch pack keygen`

Generate an ed25519 signing keypair into a local JSON file

- `--out <OUT>` ‚Äî Where to write the keypair JSON

## `nuthatch pack verify`

Verify a pack: signature, artifact hashes, and grant conformance

- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Nest directory (must contain a `compliance-pack.toml`)

## `nuthatch roost`

Run a roost: many nests on one chain behind one API, each under `/<name>/‚Ä¶` (RFC-0012)


## `nuthatch roost dev`

Bring up every nest a `roost.toml` mounts and serve them behind one listener: `/nests` roster plus each nest's full API under its `/<name>/‚Ä¶` prefix. Chain identity is shared; the stores are per-nest and isolated

- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Roost directory (must contain a roost.toml and a nests/ dir)
- `--listen <LISTEN>` ‚Äî Address to bind the HTTP API to
- `--rpc <RPC>` ‚Äî Override the roost's `rpc_urls` at runtime without editing the config (repeatable)
- `--backfill <BACKFILL>` ‚Äî Index only this many blocks back from the tip, for every mounted nest (recent-history mode)
- `--seal-direct` ‚Äî Backfill finalized history straight to Parquet before tip-following, for every nest (RFC-0004)
- `--concurrency <CONCURRENCY>` ‚Äî Concurrent window fetches during each nest's seal-direct backfill
- `--window <WINDOW>` ‚Äî Override the `eth_getLogs` block-window (the chain default otherwise) for every nest's backfill
- `--no-admin` ‚Äî Disable the built-in admin UI (`/<name>/_admin/`) entirely for every nest (RFC-0010 Part A)

## `nuthatch schema`

Regenerate the derived artifacts (`schema.json`, `llms.txt`, `semantic.toml` footguns) from `nuthatch.toml` ‚Äî run after hand-editing the config (e.g. adding a factory `[[template]]`), so the schema and the derived `*_dec` columns match the tables the config now produces

- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Nest directory (must contain a `nuthatch.toml`)

## `nuthatch screen`

Screen sealed transfers against a list snapshot, recording `sanction_hit` annotations (RFC-0008 C2). Replayable: same list hash + range + component ‚Üí identical hits

- `--list <LIST>` ‚Äî The list snapshot hash to screen against (from `nuthatch lists fetch`)
- `--from <FROM>` ‚Äî First block of the range to screen (inclusive)
- `--to <TO>` ‚Äî Last block of the range to screen (inclusive)
- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Nest directory (must contain a `nuthatch.toml` and sealed segments over the range)

## `nuthatch sql`

Query a nest's data with SQL ‚Äî the live tip and sealed history, one surface. Prints a table

- `<QUERY>` ‚Äî The SQL query (SELECT/WITH). Tables are `{alias}__{event}`, e.g. `usdc__transfer`. Omit to open an interactive REPL (`.tables`, `.schema <t>`, history; `.exit` to quit)
- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Nest directory (queried directly when no `nuthatch dev` holds the store)
- `--url <URL>` ‚Äî The running instance's API, used when the local store is locked by `nuthatch dev`
- `--json` ‚Äî Emit newline-delimited JSON instead of a table (for piping to jq etc.)

## `nuthatch transform`

Run a WASM transform component over a project's stored transfers

- `<COMPONENT>` ‚Äî Path to the transform component (.wasm, wasm32-wasip2)
- `--dir <DIR>` ‚Äî Project directory (must contain a nuthatch.redb with indexed transfers)
- `--limit <LIMIT>` ‚Äî How many of the most-recent transfers to feed the transform
